core_mechanism: Certifying that two structurally distinct, non-isomorphic objects are equivalent at the level of their associated derived (triangulated) categories of representations rather than by any direct point-to-point correspondence — a categorical/homological duality (homological projective duality) that identifies as 'the same' the different limiting descriptions reached by continuation across a parameter/moduli space.
brief_reason: The abstract's central claim is 'twisted derived equivalences between non-birational spaces' whose distinct Kahler phases are related by Kuznetsov's homological projective duality, i.e. objects with no underlying point-wise map are shown equivalent via their derived categories.
